core_mechanism: an effective-resistance / electrical-network bound on a symmetric positive-semidefinite matrix treated as a graph Laplacian: the quadratic form of the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse against a difference-of-basis-vectors (the resistance distance between two nodes) is bounded by the sum of reciprocal diagonal conductances, which is then converted into a Loewner-order (positive-semidefinite) matrix inequality
brief_reason: The abstract's main result bounds (e_i - e_j)^T Σ^† (e_i - e_j) ≤ 1/v_i + 1/v_j and derives the Loewner-order corollary Σ ⪰ (1/2)(D - vv^⊤/V), which is exactly the effective-resistance-≤-sum-of-reciprocal-conductances inequality for a Laplacian pseudoinverse turned into a PSD relaxation.
